hypothesis: Incorporating molecular charge as a model input feature—identified in the roadmap as a planned extension—will disproportionately improve MD simulation stability and force prediction accuracy for the subset of drug-like molecules containing titratable groups (e.g., amines, carboxylic acids) compared to neutral organic scaffolds, such that a charge-conditioned MACE model trained on a dataset spanning pH-relevant protonation states will reduce per-atom force MAE by at least 30% on solvated charged species relative to the current neutral-only pre-trained models, while producing no statistically significant improvement on strictly neutral, non-titratable molecules.
